name: Fertilization & Early Development
description: 20 illustrated MCQs on fertilization, cleavage, the germ layers and implantation.
author: MJ Technology Solutions

questions.question: Fertilization occurs when a sperm and an oocyte combine and their nuclei:
questions.explanation: Fertilization occurs when a sperm and oocyte combine and their haploid nuclei fuse.

questions.question: The single cell formed immediately after the fusion of sperm and egg is called the:
questions.explanation: The fused sperm and egg form a diploid single cell called the zygote.

questions.question: A zygote is a diploid cell, meaning it contains:
options: Twice the normal number of chromosomes
A full set of chromosomes (46 in humans)
questions.explanation: Combining two haploid gametes forms a diploid zygote with the full 46 chromosomes.

questions.question: In which anatomical location does fertilization of the ovum normally occur?
questions.explanation: Fertilization normally occurs in the ampulla of the uterine tube.
questions.imageAlt: Fertilization in the fallopian tube and journey to the uterus

questions.question: The whole fertilization process lasts approximately:
questions.explanation: Fertilization is completed within about 24 hours of ovulation.

questions.question: Which enzyme, released from the sperm acrosome, helps it penetrate the corona radiata?
questions.explanation: Hyaluronidase from the acrosome helps the sperm penetrate the corona radiata.

questions.question: The enzymes acrosin and neuraminidase enable the sperm to penetrate the:
questions.explanation: Acrosin and neuraminidase cause lysis of the zona pellucida, forming a path to the oocyte.
questions.imageAlt: Structure of the human egg (ovum) with zona pellucida and corona radiata

questions.question: After one sperm penetrates, the change that makes the zona pellucida impermeable to other sperm is the:
questions.explanation: The zona reaction makes the zona pellucida impermeable to additional sperm, preventing polyspermy.

questions.question: Entry of the sperm triggers the secondary oocyte to complete its:
questions.explanation: After sperm entry, the secondary oocyte completes its second meiotic division, forming the ovum and a polar body.

questions.question: The nucleus of the mature oocyte after sperm entry is known as the:
questions.explanation: The nucleus of the mature oocyte becomes the female pronucleus.

questions.question: Each pronucleus contains how many chromosomes before they fuse?
questions.explanation: Each pronucleus has 23 chromosomes, so the zygote ends up with 46 in total.

questions.question: Which is NOT one of the main results of fertilization?
options: Determination of the sex of the embryo
Restoration of the diploid chromosome number
questions.explanation: Fertilization restores diploidy, determines sex and initiates cleavage; placenta forms later from the trophoblast.

questions.question: The mitotic divisions that increase cell number without increasing overall size are called:
questions.explanation: Cleavage divisions increase cell number while the overall size stays the same.

questions.question: The individual cells produced by cleavage of the zygote are known as:
questions.explanation: The cells produced by cleavage are called blastomeres.

questions.question: A solid ball of cells (about the 32-cell stage) with an inner and outer cell mass is the:
questions.explanation: At the 32-cell stage the embryo is a morula — a solid ball with inner cell mass and trophoblast.

questions.question: The outer cell mass (trophoblast) of the developing embryo will eventually form part of the:
questions.explanation: The trophoblast (outer cell mass) becomes part of the placenta; the inner cell mass becomes the embryo.

questions.question: During gastrulation, how many germ cell layers are formed?
questions.explanation: Gastrulation forms three germ layers: ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm.
questions.imageAlt: Blastula and gastrula with the three germ layers

questions.question: The nervous system and the epidermis of the skin develop from which germ layer?
questions.explanation: The ectoderm (outer layer) forms the nervous system and epidermis of the skin.

questions.question: Bones, muscles, blood and blood vessels develop mainly from which germ layer?
questions.explanation: The mesoderm (middle layer) forms bones, muscles, blood, blood vessels and the reproductive/excretory systems.

questions.question: Implantation of the blastocyst into the endometrium begins around:
questions.explanation: Implantation of the blastocyst into the endometrium occurs between day 7 and 8, finishing around day 14.
questions.imageAlt: Timeline of fertilization, cleavage and implantation
